findings.title: Overview clearly frames mission, modes, and delegation
findings.detail: Seven-line Overview covers the Quality Guardian role, five review dimensions, delegation to quality-control for deterministic work, dual default vs ad-hoc behavior, and human calibration. Matches outcome-driven guidance for a domain-heavy gate agent; no overlap with defensive padding per pre-pass (zero waste_pattern hits).

findings.title: Human reviewer name hardcoded as Juan while config uses user_name
findings.detail: On Activation resolves {user_name} from config, but Identity, Communication Style, and Principles use the concrete name Juan. This breaks portability across projects and conflicts with the stated config-driven communication pattern. Not persona waste—the intent is calibration examples—but the implementation should stay consistent with variables.
findings.action: Replace Juan with {user_name} (with null-safe phrasing) or neutral wording such as "the human reviewer" wherever reviewer-specific examples appear (also lines 29 and 38).

findings.title: Suggestive loading for patterns.md and chronology.md
findings.detail: Sections use "Load when needed" rather than mandatory triggers. Under context compaction or rushed execution, the agent may skip pattern and history files when calibration or recurring-issue reporting depends on them. This is progressive disclosure tradeoff—not always wrong—but weaker than imperative load rules for judgment-heavy memory use.
findings.action: Replace with explicit when-to-load rules (e.g., load patterns.md before emitting pattern_alerts or after N reviews; load chronology.md when reporting calibration_notes).

findings.title: Hardcoded reviewer name in memory-system reference
findings.detail: Same Juan hardcoding as SKILL.md appears in patterns.md description. Keeps sidecar docs aligned with one install only.
findings.action: Use {user_name} or generic "human reviewer" in reference text.

findings.title: No explicit progression or continuation conditions in SKILL.md
findings.detail: Pre-pass reported has_progression false. Headless vs interactive paths are described, but there are no stated gates (e.g., when to pause for partial artifacts, resume multi-artifact review, or require human ack) comparable to step-workflow skills. For typical single-shot Marcus handoffs this is acceptable; risk grows for long or interrupted reviews.
findings.action: If multi-phase reviews are common, add a short subsection with explicit continuation conditions; otherwise document that single-pass completion is intentional.

findings.title: Capabilities table uses imperative Load routes to references
findings.detail: Internal capabilities map to ./references/review-protocol.md, feedback-format.md, and save-memory.md with direct Load instructions—not optional "see if needed" language. Supports self-containment and context survival when those files are loaded per activation.

skillmd_assessment.notes: 114 lines, ~3k tokens: well within guidelines. Heavy procedural and persona content is load-bearing for a QA gate agent. Procedure depth lives in references/review-protocol.md and feedback-format.md appropriately; no large inline schemas in SKILL.md.

summary.assessment: Strong prompt craft for a single-SKILL agent: clear Overview, identity, and outcome-focused principles; progressive disclosure to references is appropriate. Pre-pass showed no defensive padding or back-references. Main gaps are config consistency (hardcoded reviewer name) and mildly suggestive memory loading phrasing.
summary.top_improvement: Align all reviewer-facing examples and reference text with {user_name} (or neutral phrasing) to match On Activation config loading.
